name: Daily Challenge
description: Daily Challenge is a profile-level mechanic that operates across sessions, runs, or modes rather than only inside one encounter.
design_notes: Daily Challenge should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For meta mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use daily challenge as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect daily challenge to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels their history across sessions has visible meaning.

parameters.name: reset_period
parameters.description: When the mechanic refreshes or resets.

parameters.name: reward_tier_count
parameters.description: Number of visible reward or stat tiers.

parameters.name: visibility_rule
parameters.description: When the player can see the meta information.

edge_cases: Daily Challenge is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Daily Challenge overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Daily Challenge runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Daily Challenge state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Daily Challenge plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Daily Challenge produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Daily Challenge strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Daily Challenge becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Daily Challenge with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Daily Challenge as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Daily Challenge with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Daily Challenge as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
